## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

4e7b5f29  ₢BBAAs    enshrine simplify: remove BBAA0 classifier overload, drop orchestration's enshrine-airgap-validate call. The classifier conflated two qualitatively different jobs into a single tabtarget: (1) mirror upstream + write back ANCHOR (forge case), (2) HEAD-validate orchestration-pinned hallmark (airgap-bottle case). Refining the switch was wrong-direction; the right move was to remove the overload so each job has a clean home. The HEAD-200 'skip' path was framed as an optimization but actively breaks enshrine's contract: HEAD-200 on a cached locator proves only that some manifest exists at that path, not that it matches current upstream. Skipping based on cache reachability silently locks the vessel to a stale digest, hiding the upstream-rotated case (the case Brad's earlier commit 14242011 records empirically when rust:slim-bookworm digest moved 5ae2d2ef98 to caaf9ca7ac). rbfd_enshrine now: (a) requires at least one slot has RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN populated (ANCHOR-only is no longer a valid input shape; ANCHOR is a derived artifact of this operation, not a pin); (b) unconditionally submits the Cloud Build job that inspects current upstream, computes the content-addressed locator from the live manifest digest, and copies into GAR; (c) extract overwrites whatever was previously committed in RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR. Re-runs are idempotent w.r.t. upstream state, not w.r.t. the file's prior contents — that distinction was the original design mistake. Internal pipeline: zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots removed entirely (84 lines), zrbfd_enshrine_submit's classification-file read loop replaced with direct ${RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN:-} reads. Empty ORIGIN slots become empty CB substitutions; the CB step's existing `test -n ${ORIGIN} || continue` guard skips them naturally. Orchestration: rbtdro_onboarding.rs drops the enshrine-airgap-validate call after the airgap-bottle anchor write; the forge hallmark's existence in GAR is established by ordain-forge's success above, no separate validation step needed. airgap_sigil local removed (now unused). 4e7b5f29  ₢BBAAs    enshrine simplify: remove BBAA0 classifier overload, drop orchestration's enshrine-airgap-validate call. The classifier conflated two qualitatively different jobs into a single tabtarget: (1) mirror upstream + write back ANCHOR (forge case), (2) HEAD-validate orchestration-pinned hallmark (airgap-bottle case). Refining the switch was wrong-direction; the right move was to remove the overload so each job has a clean home. The HEAD-200 'skip' path was framed as an optimization but actively breaks enshrine's contract: HEAD-200 on a cached locator proves only that some manifest exists at that path, not that it matches current upstream. Skipping based on cache reachability silently locks the vessel to a stale digest, hiding the upstream-rotated case (the case Brad's earlier commit 14242011 records empirically when rust:slim-bookworm digest moved 5ae2d2ef98 to caaf9ca7ac). rbfd_enshrine now: (a) requires at least one slot has RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN populated (ANCHOR-only is no longer a valid input shape; ANCHOR is a derived artifact of this operation, not a pin); (b) unconditionally submits the Cloud Build job that inspects current upstream, computes the content-addressed locator from the live manifest digest, and copies into GAR; (c) extract overwrites whatever was previously committed in RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR. Re-runs are idempotent w.r.t. upstream state, not w.r.t. the file's prior contents — that distinction was the original design mistake. 1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Gauntlet test suite — design
55	
56	`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.
57	
58	**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.
59	
60	**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).
61	
62	**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:
63	
64	1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
65	2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
66	3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
67	4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
68	5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
69	6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
70	7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)
71	
72	No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.
73	
74	## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage
75	
76	The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.
77	
78	Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.
79	
80	**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.
81	
82	Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.
83	
84	Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.
85	
86	## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing
87	
88	Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:
89	
90	- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
91	- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.
92	
93	**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.
94	
95	Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.
96	
97	**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.
98	
99	## Suite-level fail-fast
100	
101	Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.
102	
103	## No-teardown decision
104	
105	The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:
106	
107	- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
108	- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
109	- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
110	- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.
111	
112	Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).
113	
114	## Operator scope
115	
116	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.
117	
118	## Coupling to ₣A_
119	
120	Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.
121	
122	## Locked design decisions
123	
124	- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
125	- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
126	- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.
127	
128	## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh
129	
130	Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.
131	
132	**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.
133	
134	**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):
135	
136	- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
137	- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
138	- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
139	- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.
140	
141	Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.
142	
143	Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.
144	
145	## References
146	
147	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
148	- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
149	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
150	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
151	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
152	
153	# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAs
154	
155	## Character
156	
157	Empirical run-until-green for `onboarding-sequence` against a canonical-established depot.
158	
159	## Iteration loop
160	
161	Each turn:
162	
163	1. Run the fixture, or the named failing case.
164	2. If needed, use `gcloud` to diagnose.
165	3. Recommend a fix.
166	4. Await guidance.
167	
168	No autonomous re-runs. No autonomous edits.
169	
170	## In-flight progress
171	
172	Empirical run on 2026-04-29 (post `c547c62a`) was stopped mid-fixture by operator (had to leave). Cases that passed before stop:
173	
174	1. `inscribe-reliquary` PASSED
175	2. `kludge-tadmor` PASSED — first green confirmation of the kludge/ordain self-veto fixes (`142cf920` + `c547c62a`).
176	3. `kludge-ccyolo` PASSED.
177	
178	Cases not yet observed in this run: `conjure-srjcl`, `ordain-airgap`, `propagate-moriah`, `ordain-pluml`, `ordain-graft`. Next mount: re-run from the top against still-valid canonical depot+SAs (case probes will gate); if precondition state still holds, advance through the remaining cases and diagnose the first new failure.
179	
180	## Wrap criterion
181	
182	Green tally on onboarding-sequence.
183	
184	## Cross-pace dependencies
185	
186	- BBAAu landed.
187	- canonical-establish (BBAAe) for depot+SAs.
188	
189	## Out of scope
190	
191	- Construction — BBAAu.
192	- Full gauntlet — BBAAX.
193	- moriah Fixture static — phantom.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Gauntlet test suite — design

`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.

**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.

**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).

**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:

1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)

No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.

## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage

The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.

Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.

**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.

Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.

Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.

## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing

Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:

- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.

**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.

Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.

**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.

## Suite-level fail-fast

Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.

## No-teardown decision

The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:

- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.

Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.

## Locked design decisions

- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.

## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh

Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.

**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.

**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):

- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.

Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.

Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

Pausing. Here's the failure analysis and a recommendation pending your guidance.

**Failure**: `ordain-bind` on `rbev-bottle-plantuml` aborts in `rbfd_ordain` with `RBRV_RELIQUARY is required — run inscribe to create a reliquary first`.

**Tally**: 6 passed, 1 failed, 0 skipped (`ordain-pluml`, `ordain-graft` never ran due to suite-internal fail-fast).

**Key passes worth noting**:
- `ordain-airgap` PASSED — the BBAAs enshrine simplification (4e7b5f29) is empirically green.
- `kludge-tadmor` and `kludge-ccyolo` PASSED — kludge/ordain self-veto fixes hold.

**Design tension surfaced** — `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdro_onboarding.rs:99-109` documents the intent:

> `rbev-bottle-plantuml has RBRV_RELIQUARY= as schema parity but is bind-mode. Excluded from yoke fan-out — bind mode does not use the reliquary.`

So orchestration **deliberately** does not yoke into plantuml. But `rbfd_ordain` is failing the bind-mode vessel for the field being empty. The orchestration's stated design contract and the producer's precondition contract disagree on whether RBRV_RELIQUARY is required for bind-mode.

**Three candidate resolutions** (each consistent with a different load-bearing reading; not pre-deciding):

1. **Loosen `rbfd_ordain`** — make `RBRV_RELIQUARY` required only for conjure/airgap modes; bind/graft tolerate empty. Aligns with orchestration's documented intent.
2. **Yoke bind/graft anyway** — drop the exclusion; have orchestration yoke into all ordain-path vessels regardless of mode. The comment is wrong; bind/graft simply ignore the field.
3. **Drop the field from bind/graft `rbrv.env`** — remove `RBRV_RELIQUARY=` entirely from plantuml/graft-demo. Then `rbfd_ordain` would have to read it as "absent → not required" rather than "empty → required."

Question that decides it: does `rbfd_ordain` actually need the reliquary stamp to run a bind-mode build? If no, then (1) is the cheapest fix. If somehow yes (e.g., for SBOM tagging), then (2) or (3).

I have not opened `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` to inspect the exact gate location yet — pausing here per the docket's "await guidance" rule. Tell me which direction to investigate.

# Verify reliquary tool images exist in GAR.
# Full-subtree presence check: every canonical tool image must be present at
# the stamp. Inscribe creates all 6 atomically; piecemeal jettison or registry
# damage compromises the stamp's integrity. Misses are accumulated across the
# whole tool set so the operator sees full damage in one report.
# Args: token vessel_dir
zrbfd_preflight_reliquary() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_token}"      || buc_die "zrbfd_preflight_reliquary: token required"
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "zrbfd_preflight_reliquary: vessel_dir required"

  local -r z_reliquary="${RBRV_RELIQUARY:-}"
  test -n "${z_reliquary}" || return 0

  buc_step "Verifying reliquary tool images exist in GAR"

  local -r z_canonical_tools=(
    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD}"
    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER}"
    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE}"
    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT}"
    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT}"
    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO}"
  )

  local z_missing=()
  local z_tool=""
  local z_pkg=""
  local z_status_file=""
  local z_response_file=""
  local z_stderr_file=""
  local z_http_code=""

  for z_tool in "${z_canonical_tools[@]}"; do
    z_pkg="${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_reliquary}/${z_tool}"
    z_status_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}reliquary_${z_tool}_status.txt"
    z_response_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}reliquary_${z_tool}_response.txt"
    z_stderr_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}reliquary_${z_tool}_stderr.txt"

    curl --head -sS \
      --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
      --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
      -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
      -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
      -w "%{http_code}" \
      -o "${z_response_file}" \
      "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg}/manifests/${z_reliquary}" \
      > "${z_status_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
      || buc_die "HEAD request failed for reliquary tool: ${z_pkg}:${z_reliquary} — see ${z_stderr_file}"

    z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
    test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for reliquary check: ${z_tool}"

    case "${z_http_code}" in
      200) buc_log_args "Reliquary tool present: ${z_tool}" ;;
      404) z_missing+=("${z_tool}") ;;
      *)   buc_die "Unexpected HTTP ${z_http_code} when checking reliquary tool: ${z_pkg}:${z_reliquary}" ;;
    esac
  done

  if test "${#z_missing[@]}" -eq 0; then
    buc_info "Reliquary verified: ${z_reliquary} (${#z_canonical_tools[@]}/${#z_canonical_tools[@]} tools present)"
    return 0
  fi

  buc_warn "Reliquary integrity check failed: ${z_reliquary} (${#z_missing[@]}/${#z_canonical_tools[@]} tools missing)"
  buc_bare "  The reliquary is a co-versioned set of builder tool images (gcloud, docker,"
  buc_bare "  syft, alpine, binfmt, skopeo) inscribed from upstream into your private GAR."
  buc_bare "  Air-gapped worker pools cannot pull from the public internet — the reliquary"
  buc_bare "  stages these tools so builds can run without egress. Piecemeal jettison is"
  buc_bare "  allowed but unrecoverable surgically: re-inscribe the whole stamp."
  buc_bare ""
  for z_tool in "${z_missing[@]}"; do
    buc_bare "  PRECHECK: GAR image not found at ${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_reliquary}/${z_tool}"
    buc_bare "    Required by ${RBRV_SIGIL}'s RBRV_RELIQUARY=${z_reliquary}."
  done
  buc_bare ""
  buc_bare "  Recover by re-inscribing the reliquary, then re-yoking and re-ordaining:"
  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY}"
  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY}" "<new-stamp>" "${z_vessel_dir}"
  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
  buc_die "Registry preflight failed — ${#z_missing[@]} of ${#z_canonical_tools[@]} reliquary tool images missing from GAR"
}


# Check concurrent build quota against regime requirements
# Args: token mode
#   mode: "gate" (die if insufficient) or "advisory" (warn if insufficient)
zrbfd_quota_preflight() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"

  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbfd_quota_preflight: token required"

  # Extract vCPU count from machine type (last segment after final hyphen)
  local -r z_vcpus="${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE##*-}"
  case "${z_vcpus}" in
    ""|0|*[!0-9]*)
      buc_warn "Cannot parse vCPU count from RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE='${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE}' -- skipping quota preflight"
      return 0
      ;;
  esac

  buc_log_args "Machine type ${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE} = ${z_vcpus} vCPUs"

  # Query Service Usage consumer quota API for concurrent_private_pool_build_cpus
  local -r z_metric_encoded="cloudbuild.googleapis.com%2Fconcurrent_private_pool_build_cpus"
  local -r z_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_SERVICEUSAGE}${RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1BETA1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/services/cloudbuild.googleapis.com/consumerQuotaMetrics/${z_metric_encoded}"

  buc_step "Checking concurrent build quota"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}" "quota_preflight"

  local z_code=""
  z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "quota_preflight") || z_code=""
  if test "${z_code}" != "200"; then
    buc_warn "Could not query build quota (HTTP ${z_code}) -- skipping preflight check"
    return 0
  fi

  # Filter quota response to region-specific bucket via intermediate file
  rbgu_jq_file_to_file_ok "quota_preflight" "quota_region" \
    "[.consumerQuotaLimits[0].quotaBuckets[] | select(.dimensions.region == \"${RBRR_GCP_REGION}\")] | .[0] // {}" \
    || true

  # Extract effective limit from region bucket, then fallback to first bucket
  local z_limit=""
  z_limit=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "quota_region" '.effectiveLimit') || z_limit=""
  if test -z "${z_limit}"; then
    z_limit=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "quota_preflight" \
      '.consumerQuotaLimits[0].quotaBuckets[0].effectiveLimit') || z_limit=""
  fi

  if test -z "${z_limit}"; then
    buc_warn "Could not extract quota limit -- skipping preflight check"
    return 0
  fi

  # -1 means unlimited
  if test "${z_limit}" = "-1"; then
    buc_info "Quota: unlimited concurrent private pool build CPUs"
    return 0
  fi

  # Compute max concurrent builds
  local -r z_max_concurrent=$((z_limit / z_vcpus))

  buc_log_args "Quota ${z_limit} vCPUs, machine ${z_vcpus} vCPUs, max concurrent ${z_max_concurrent}, required ${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS}"

  if test "${z_max_concurrent}" -lt "${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS}"; then
    buc_warn "Build quota insufficient: ${z_limit} vCPU quota / ${z_vcpus} vCPUs per build = ${z_max_concurrent} concurrent (need ${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS})"
    buc_warn "Fresh depots start with a low quota. After some build activity, the Edit Quotas option becomes available."
    buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_QUOTA_BUILD}"
  else
    buc_info "Quota OK: ${z_limit} vCPU / ${z_vcpus} per build = ${z_max_concurrent} concurrent (need ${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS})"
  fi
}

# Internal: Verify that all required images exist in GAR before submitting
# to Cloud Build. Checks two layers in dependency order:
#
#   1. Reliquary — co-versioned builder tool images (gcloud, docker, syft, etc.)
#      created by inscribe. One reliquary per depot setup. Enshrine depends on it.
#   2. Enshrine — upstream base images copied into private GAR, pinned by content
#      hash. One enshrine per base image anchor. Multiple vessels sharing the same
#      anchor only need one enshrine.
#
# A missing image at either layer causes a Cloud Build failure minutes later.
# This preflight catches it immediately with copy-paste remediation commands.
#
# Must be called after vessel load (reads RBRV_RELIQUARY, RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR)
# and authentication (needs token for registry API).
zrbfd_registry_preflight() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_token}"      || buc_die "zrbfd_registry_preflight: token required"
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "zrbfd_registry_preflight: vessel_dir required"

  # --- Layer 1: Reliquary tool images ---
  zrbfd_preflight_reliquary "${z_token}" "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # --- Layer 2: Enshrined base images ---
  # Each vessel declares base images via RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN (upstream tag) and
  # RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR (locator: package-path:tag). Enshrine copies the upstream
  # image into the enshrine namespace, pinned by content hash. The conjure
  # Dockerfile's FROM references the locator-resolved GAR ref, not upstream tag.

  buc_step "Verifying enshrined base images exist in GAR"

  local z_n=""
  local z_anchor_var=""
  local z_anchor=""
  local z_pkg_path=""
  local z_tag=""

local z_self_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
  readonly ZRBFC_RBGJA_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgja"
  test -d "${ZRBFC_RBGJA_STEPS_DIR}" || buc_die "RBGJA steps directory not found: ${ZRBFC_RBGJA_STEPS_DIR}"
  readonly ZRBFC_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgjv"
  test -d "${ZRBFC_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR}" || buc_die "RBGJV steps directory not found: ${ZRBFC_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR}"

  buc_log_args 'Tool image refs — mutable kindle state, populated by zrbfc_resolve_tool_images'
  z_rbfc_tool_gcloud=""
  z_rbfc_tool_docker=""
  z_rbfc_tool_alpine=""
  z_rbfc_tool_syft=""
  z_rbfc_tool_binfmt=""
  z_rbfc_tool_skopeo=""

  readonly ZRBFC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbfc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBFC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfc not kindled - call zrbfc_kindle first"
}

# Internal: Resolve tool image references from reliquary.
# Must be called after vessel load (reads RBRV_RELIQUARY).
# Sets module-level z_rbfc_tool_* mutable kindle state for downstream step assembly.
# Idempotent — safe to call multiple times per invocation.
zrbfc_resolve_tool_images() {
  zrbfc_sentinel

  local -r z_reliquary="${RBRV_RELIQUARY:-}"
  test -n "${z_reliquary}" \
    || buc_die "RBRV_RELIQUARY is required — run inscribe to create a reliquary first"

  local -r z_rqy_prefix="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_reliquary}"
  z_rbfc_tool_gcloud="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD}:${z_reliquary}"
  z_rbfc_tool_docker="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER}:${z_reliquary}"
  z_rbfc_tool_alpine="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE}:${z_reliquary}"
  z_rbfc_tool_syft="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT}:${z_reliquary}"
  z_rbfc_tool_binfmt="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT}:${z_reliquary}"
  z_rbfc_tool_skopeo="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO}:${z_reliquary}"
  buc_log_args "Tool images resolved from reliquary: ${z_reliquary}"
}

# Validate that a vessel sigil is non-empty and corresponds to a known vessel.
# On missing or invalid sigil, lists available vessels and dies.
# Does NOT resolve to a directory — use zrbfc_resolve_vessel for that.
rbfc_require_vessel_sigil() {
  zrbfc_sentinel

  local -r z_sigil="${1:-}"

  if test -n "${z_sigil}" && test -d "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_sigil}" && test -f "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_sigil}/rbrv.env"; then
    return 0
  fi

  local z_sigils=""
  z_sigils=$(rbrv_list_capture) || buc_die "No vessels found"
  buc_step "Available vessels:"
  local z_s=""
  for z_s in ${z_sigils}; do
    buc_bare "        ${z_s}"
  done
  if test -z "${z_sigil}"; then
    buc_die "Vessel parameter required"
  fi
  buc_die "Vessel not found: ${z_sigil}"
}

# Resolve vessel argument: accepts a sigil (e.g., rbev-sentry-deb-tether) or a path
# (e.g., rbev-vessels/rbev-sentry-deb-tether).  On no-arg or invalid arg, lists
# available vessels and dies.  On success, writes resolved path to ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE.
zrbfc_resolve_vessel() {
  zrbfc_sentinel

  local -r z_arg="${1:-}"

  # Try as path first, then as sigil under RBRR_VESSEL_DIR
  if test -n "${z_arg}" && test -d "${z_arg}" && test -f "${z_arg}/rbrv.env"; then
    printf '%s' "${z_arg}" > "${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write resolved vessel path"
    return 0

rbfd_ordain() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Ordain a hallmark from a vessel (conjure, mirror, or graft based on vessel mode)"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path)
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${1:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"

  # Peek at vessel mode without sourcing (sourcing makes vars readonly,
  # and the downstream function will source again via zrbfc_load_vessel)
  local -r z_rbrv_file="${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"
  local z_mode=""
  local z_mode_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_mode_line || test -n "${z_mode_line}"; do
    case "${z_mode_line}" in
      RBRV_VESSEL_MODE=*) z_mode="${z_mode_line#RBRV_VESSEL_MODE=}"; break ;;
    esac
  done < "${z_rbrv_file}"
  z_mode="${z_mode:-conjure}"
  case "${z_mode}" in
    conjure) rbfd_build "${z_vessel_dir}" ;;
    bind)    rbfd_mirror "${z_vessel_dir}" ;;
    graft)   rbfd_graft "${z_vessel_dir}" ;;
    *)       buc_die "Unknown vessel mode: ${z_mode}" ;;
  esac

  # Chaining: read hallmark persisted by mode dispatch
  buc_step "Reading hallmark from mode dispatch output"
  local z_hallmark=""
  z_hallmark=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to read hallmark from output"
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Empty hallmark in output"

  # Metadata pipeline: graft uses combined about+vouch; conjure/bind already have about, need standalone vouch
  case "${z_mode}" in
    conjure)
      buc_info "About produced by combined conjure job — proceeding to vouch"
      rbfv_vouch "${z_vessel_dir}" "${z_hallmark}"
      ;;
    graft)
      zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit "${z_vessel_dir}" "${z_hallmark}"
      ;;
    bind)
      buc_info "About produced by combined bind job — proceeding to vouch"
      rbfv_vouch "${z_vessel_dir}" "${z_hallmark}"
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "Unknown vessel mode in chaining: ${z_mode}"
      ;;
  esac
}

rbfd_build() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local -r z_vessel_dir="${1:-}"

  # Documentation block
  buc_doc_brief "Build container image from vessel via direct builds.create submission"
  buc_doc_param "vessel_dir" "Path to vessel directory containing rbrv.env"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_log_args "Validate parameters"
  if test -z "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
    local z_sigils
    z_sigils=$(rbrv_list_capture) || buc_die "No vessels found"
    buc_step "Available vessels:"
    local z_sigil=""
    for z_sigil in ${z_sigils}; do
      buc_bare "        ${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_sigil}"
    done
    buc_die "Vessel directory required"
  fi

  # Load and validate vessel

  local z_context_tag=""
  z_context_tag=$(<"${ZRBFD_CONTEXT_PREFIX}tag.txt")
  test -n "${z_context_tag}" || buc_die "Empty context image tag after push"

  # Stitch build JSON — generates complete builds.create resource directly
  buc_step "Stitching build JSON"
  local -r z_build_file="${ZRBFD_CONTEXT_PREFIX}build.json"
  zrbfd_stitch_build_json "${z_build_file}" "${z_hallmark}" "${z_context_tag}"

  # Submit via builds.create (no source — context delivered via GAR image)
  buc_step "Submitting build via builds.create"
  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${ZRBFC_GCB_PROJECT_BUILDS_URL}" "${z_token}" \
    "build_direct_create" "${z_build_file}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Direct build submission" "build_direct_create"

  # Extract build ID from Operation response
  local z_build_id=""
  z_build_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "build_direct_create" '.metadata.build.id') || z_build_id=""
  test -n "${z_build_id}" || buc_die "Build ID not found in builds.create response"
  echo "${z_build_id}" > "${ZRBFC_BUILD_ID_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to persist build ID"

rbfd_mirror() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local z_vessel_dir="${1:-}"

  # Documentation block
  buc_doc_brief "Mirror a bind vessel image from upstream to GAR via combined Cloud Build (skopeo copy + about)"
  buc_doc_param "vessel_dir" "Path to vessel directory containing rbrv.env"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # No-arg: list available vessels
  if test -z "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
    local z_sigils
    z_sigils=$(rbrv_list_capture) || buc_die "No vessels found"
    buc_step "Available vessels:"
    local z_sigil=""
    for z_sigil in ${z_sigils}; do
      buc_bare "        ${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_sigil}"
    done
    buc_die "Vessel directory required"
  fi

  # Load and validate vessel
  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-}" = "bind" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' is not a bind vessel (mode: ${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-unset})"
  test -n "${RBRV_BIND_IMAGE:-}" \
    || buc_die "RBRV_BIND_IMAGE not set for bind vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}'"

  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (mirror uses skopeo + about steps from reliquary)
  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images

  # Dirty-tree guard (same as inscribe — mirror should match a committed state)
  buc_step "Verifying clean working tree"
  git diff --quiet \
    || buc_die "Working tree has unstaged changes — commit before mirroring"
  git diff --cached --quiet \
    || buc_die "Index has staged changes — commit before mirroring"

  # Authenticate as Director
  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # Registry preflight -- verify reliquary and enshrined base images exist before expensive operations
  zrbfd_registry_preflight "${z_token}" "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # GAR coordinates
  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  local -r z_gar_base="${z_gar_host}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"

  # Generate hallmark timestamps: bYYMMDDHHMMSS-rYYMMDDHHMMSS
  local -r z_mirror_ts="b${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
  local -r z_build_ts_file="${ZRBFD_MIRROR_PREFIX}build_ts.txt"
  date -u +'%y%m%d%H%M%S' > "${z_build_ts_file}" || buc_die "Failed to generate build timestamp"
  local z_build_ts
  z_build_ts="r$(<"${z_build_ts_file}")"
  test -n "${z_build_ts}" || buc_die "Empty build timestamp from ${z_build_ts_file}"
  local -r z_hallmark="${z_mirror_ts}-${z_build_ts}"

  buc_info "Hallmark: ${z_hallmark}"

  # Persist to output directory for chaining by rbfd_ordain
  echo "${z_vessel_dir}" > "${ZRBFC_OUTPUT_VESSEL_DIR}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write vessel dir to output"
  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}" "${z_hallmark}"

  # Write GAR root fact file
  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_GAR_ROOT}" "${z_gar_base}"

  # Write ark stem fact file (hallmark subtree under HALLMARKS_ROOT)
  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_ARK_STEM}" "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}"

  # Write yield fact file (single-platform bind image)
  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_ARK_YIELD}-${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}" \
    "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:${z_hallmark}"

  # Submit combined Cloud Build (skopeo image copy + about steps)
  zrbfd_mirror_submit "${z_hallmark}" "${z_token}"

  # Summary
  echo ""
  buc_success "Mirror complete: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
  echo "  Hallmark: ${z_hallmark}"
}

1689:Shared fields include `vessel_mode`, `vessel_name`, `platform`, `image_digest`; conjure adds SLSA provenance fields; bind adds `bind_source`; graft adds `graft_source`.
1700:GAR top-level path token under which reliquary tool images live as basename siblings, keyed by {rbst_reliquary_stamp}.
1736:Conjure and bind each use two Cloud Build round-trips (primary job + vouch job). Graft uses one (combined about+vouch job), since the image arrives via local push rather than a Cloud Build step.
1751:* `b` — bind (mirror pinned upstream image): `b«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-r«YYMMDDHHMMSS»`
1752:* `g` — graft (local build pushed to GAR): `g«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-r«YYMMDDHHMMSS»`
1755:For conjure, bind, and graft: `«MODE»YYMMDDHHMMSS-rYYMMDDHHMMSS` where the `r` prefix means "realized" — the moment the image materialized in GAR.
1759:T1 (mode-prefixed): when the operation was initiated — inscribe for conjure, mirror for bind, OCI image creation time for graft.
1762:For graft, T1 is an intrinsic fact about the image (its `created` metadata), not an operational timestamp.
1838:Created uniformly across all three vessel modes as part of a Cloud Build job: conjure and bind embed about steps in their primary image job; graft combines about and vouch steps in a single metadata job.
1842:root — `hallmark`, `vessel_mode` (`conjure`/`bind`/`graft`), `vessel_name`, `platform`, `image_digest`, `about_timestamp`;
1846:bind `bind` object — `source` (original upstream image reference);
1847:graft `graft` object — `source` (local image reference at push time).
1874:The vouch artifact is a multi-platform `FROM scratch` container holding `vouch_summary.json` and per-platform `verify-«SUFFIX».json` files (conjure) or a single `vouch_summary.json` (bind/graft).
1889:The vouch step branches on `_RBGV_VESSEL_MODE`: conjure runs DSSE provenance verification, bind runs digest-pin comparison, graft stamps GRAFTED.
1898:  * **Builder image selection**: Step 1 (Alpine) writes attestor keys (conjure) or is a no-op (bind/graft).
1907:For conjure and bind, the new vouch content should match the existing vouch (same digests + same provenance/pin + same verifier = identical output).
1908:For graft, the GRAFTED stamp is always identical.
1909:A mismatch for conjure/bind indicates provenance change or tampering and must produce a loud alarm.
1928:The pouch is assembled on the host by the ordain pipeline and pushed to GAR before the Cloud Build job is submitted.
1930:Conjure and bind modes create a pouch; graft mode does not (the image arrives via local push, not Cloud Build).

1954:A vessel's regime file (`rbrv.env`) declares its mode, Dockerfile path (conjure), upstream reference (bind), or local image (graft), along with platform targets and build configuration.

1224	{rbtga_ark}
1225	from the repository
1226	({rbtgog_ark}),
1227	removing its image, about, and vouch artifacts as a coherent unit.

1229	include::RBSAA-ark_abjure.adoc[]

1232	//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
1233	=== {rbtgo_ark_ordain}
1234	
1235	Ordains a hallmark from a vessel — the master ceremony dispatch operation.
1236	Detects vessel mode (conjure, bind, or graft) and delegates to the appropriate
1237	{rbtga_ark}
1238	creation path, then chains metadata and vouch pipeline stages.

1241	//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
1242	=== {rbtgo_ark_conjure}
1243	
1244	Conjures an
1245	{rbtga_ark_image}
1246	by building a container image
1247	({rbtgog_ark})
1248	from a vessel specification.

1251	include::RBSAC-ark_conjure.adoc[]

1257	Enshrines upstream base images into
1258	{gar_service}
1259	({rbtgog_ark}),
1260	producing content-addressed
1261	{rbrv_image_anchor}
1262	references from
1263	{rbrv_image_origin}
1264	declarations.
1265	The
1266	{rbtr_director}
1267	submits a Cloud Build job that copies each upstream image by tag to GAR with a human-readable anchor tag, and writes the anchor back to the vessel regime.

1269	include::RBSAE-ark_enshrine.adoc[]

1276	{rbtgi_repository}
1277	({rbtgog_ark})
1278	via native docker/podman push.
1279	The image push itself is local (no Cloud Build); about and vouch follow as Cloud Build jobs.

1281	include::RBSAG-ark_graft.adoc[]

1286	
1287	Assembles the
1288	{rbtga_ark_about}
1289	metadata artifact for an existing
1290	{rbtga_ark_image}
1291	({rbtgog_ark}).

1293	{gcb_service}
1294	steps.

1297	include::RBSAB-ark_about.adoc[]

1305	({rbtgog_ark}),

1307	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
1308	that records mode-aware verification results.

1327	{rbtgi_repository}

# Internal: submit combined mirror Cloud Build job (skopeo image copy + about steps)
# Args: hallmark token
zrbfd_mirror_submit() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local -r z_hallmark="$1"
  local -r z_token="$2"

  buc_step "Constructing combined mirror Cloud Build resource"
  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  local -r z_mason_sa="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/serviceAccounts/${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}"

  # Step 0: Mirror image via skopeo
  local -r z_mscript_path="${ZRBFD_RBGJM_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjm01-mirror-image.sh"
  test -f "${z_mscript_path}" || buc_die "Mirror step script not found: ${z_mscript_path}"

  local -r z_mbody_file="${ZRBFD_MIRROR_PREFIX}mirror_body.txt"
  local -r z_mescaped_file="${ZRBFD_MIRROR_PREFIX}mirror_escaped.txt"
  local -r z_mirror_step_file="${ZRBFD_MIRROR_PREFIX}mirror_step.json"
  local -r z_mirror_step_built="${ZRBFD_MIRROR_PREFIX}mirror_step_built.json"

  buc_log_args "Reading mirror step script (skip shebang)"
  tail -n +2 "${z_mscript_path}" > "${z_mbody_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to read mirror step script"
  local z_mbody
  z_mbody=$(<"${z_mbody_file}")
  test -n "${z_mbody}" || buc_die "Empty mirror script body"

  printf '#!/bin/bash\n%s' "${z_mbody}" > "${z_mescaped_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to escape mirror script body"

  echo "[]" > "${z_mirror_step_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize mirror step JSON"
  jq \
    --arg name "${z_rbfc_tool_skopeo}" \
    --arg id "mirror-image" \
    --rawfile script "${z_mescaped_file}" \
    '. + [{name: $name, id: $id, script: $script}]' \
    "${z_mirror_step_file}" > "${z_mirror_step_built}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to build mirror step JSON"
  mv "${z_mirror_step_built}" "${z_mirror_step_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to finalize mirror step JSON"

  # Steps 1-4: About (shared with standalone about pipeline)
  local -r z_about_steps_file="${ZRBFD_MIRROR_PREFIX}about_steps.json"
  zrbfc_assemble_about_steps "${z_about_steps_file}" "${ZRBFD_MIRROR_PREFIX}about_"

  # Combine: mirror step + about steps
  local -r z_combined_steps="${ZRBFD_MIRROR_PREFIX}combined_steps.json"
  jq -s '.[0] + .[1]' "${z_mirror_step_file}" "${z_about_steps_file}" \
    > "${z_combined_steps}" || buc_die "Failed to combine mirror and about steps"

  # Git metadata (shared temp files, idempotent)
  zrbfc_ensure_git_metadata
  local z_git_commit=""
  z_git_commit=$(<"${ZRBFC_GIT_COMMIT_FILE}")
  local z_git_branch=""
  z_git_branch=$(<"${ZRBFC_GIT_BRANCH_FILE}")
  local z_git_repo=""
  z_git_repo=$(<"${ZRBFC_GIT_REPO_FILE}")

  # Mode-specific substitution values for bind
  local -r z_bind_source="${RBRV_BIND_IMAGE:-}"
  local z_dockerfile_content=""
  local -r z_dockerfile_max_bytes=4000
  if test -n "${RBRV_BIND_OPTIONAL_DOCKERFILE:-}" && test -f "${RBRV_BIND_OPTIONAL_DOCKERFILE}"; then
    local -r z_df_size_file="${ZRBFD_MIRROR_PREFIX}df_size.txt"
    wc -c < "${RBRV_BIND_OPTIONAL_DOCKERFILE}" > "${z_df_size_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to measure Dockerfile size"
    local z_df_size=""
    z_df_size=$(<"${z_df_size_file}")
    z_df_size="${z_df_size// /}"  # Strip spaces (wc output varies by platform)
    if test "${z_df_size}" -le "${z_dockerfile_max_bytes}"; then
      z_dockerfile_content=$(<"${RBRV_BIND_OPTIONAL_DOCKERFILE}")
    else
      buc_warn "Dockerfile exceeds 4KB substitution limit (${z_df_size} bytes) — recipe.txt omitted"
    fi
  fi

  # Pool routing: bind uses vessel's egress mode (tether for upstream pulls, airgap if pre-staged)
  local z_mirror_pool=""
  case "${RBRV_EGRESS_MODE}" in
    tether) z_mirror_pool="${RBDC_POOL_TETHER}" ;;
    airgap) z_mirror_pool="${RBDC_POOL_AIRGAP}" ;;
    *) buc_die "Unknown RBRV_EGRESS_MODE: ${RBRV_EGRESS_MODE}" ;;
  esac

  # Compose Build resource JSON
  buc_log_args "Composing combined mirror Build resource JSON"
  local -r z_mirror_build_file="${ZRBFD_MIRROR_PREFIX}build.json"

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_director_yoke}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation writes `RBRV_RELIQUARY=«STAMP»` into a conjure vessel's `rbrv.env` so that subsequent Cloud Build operations (conjure, enshrine) consume the named tool image set as GCB step images.
5	Both arguments are validated before any file write: the stamp against GAR (all six tool packages must be present under the reliquary subtree), the vessel against its RBRV enrollment plus a vessel-mode gate.
6	On validation failure the operation errors diagnostically without touching any file.
7	
8	The
9	{rbtr_director}
10	yokes a reliquary stamp into a vessel's regime:

rbfl_yoke() {
  zrbfl_sentinel

  local -r z_vessel="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  local -r z_stamp="${1:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Yoke a reliquary stamp into a conjure vessel's rbrv.env — validate both, then rewrite RBRV_RELIQUARY"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil — folio (e.g., rbev-sentry-deb-tether)"
  buc_doc_param "stamp"  "Reliquary datestamp (e.g., r260327172456)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_vessel}" || buc_die "Vessel sigil required (folio)"
  test -n "${z_stamp}"  || buc_die "Reliquary stamp required (arg 1)"

  local -r z_vessel_dir="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_vessel}"
  local -r z_rbrv_file="${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"

  case "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}" in
    conjure) ;;
    bind)    buc_die "Vessel '${z_vessel}' has mode=bind; Yoke only applies to conjure vessels. Bind vessels pin an upstream digest (no Cloud Build, no reliquary)." ;;
    graft)   buc_die "Vessel '${z_vessel}' has mode=graft; Yoke only applies to conjure vessels. Graft vessels push a locally-built image (no Cloud Build, no reliquary)." ;;
    *)       buc_die "Vessel '${z_vessel}' has unrecognized RBRV_VESSEL_MODE='${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}'" ;;
  esac
  buc_info "Vessel valid — mode=${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}, egress=${RBRV_EGRESS_MODE}"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  buc_step "Validating reliquary stamp: ${z_stamp}"
  local -r z_rqy_subtree="${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}/"
  local -r z_list_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages?pageSize=1000"
  local -r z_list_infix="rbfl_yoke_list"

  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_list_infix}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List reliquary packages" "${z_list_infix}"

  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_list_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}"
  local -r z_present_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_yoke_present.txt"

  jq -r --arg subtree "${z_rqy_subtree}" '
    .packages[]?.name
    | sub("^.*/packages/"; "")
    | gsub("%2F"; "/")
    | select(startswith($subtree))
    | ltrimstr($subtree)
  ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_present_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract reliquary package names"

  local z_present=()
  local z_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
    z_present+=("${z_line}")
  done < "${z_present_file}"

  local -r z_expected=(
    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD}"
    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER}"

content.text:
Investigation complete. The failure is a **three-way contradiction** between implementation, orchestration, and spec.

## The Contradiction

| Layer | Says about bind/graft + reliquary |
|-------|-----------------------------------|
| **`rbfd_mirror` (bind impl)** | line 1404-1405: "mirror uses skopeo + about steps from reliquary" — **requires** non-empty RBRV_RELIQUARY |
| **`rbfd_graft` (graft impl)** | line 1664-1665: "graft about+vouch steps use tool images" — **requires** non-empty RBRV_RELIQUARY |
| **`rbfl_yoke` (yoke impl)** | line 214-215: `buc_die`s on bind/graft with rationale "no Cloud Build, no reliquary" — **refuses to set** RBRV_RELIQUARY |
| **`RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc`** | step "Gate on Vessel Mode" lines 31-44: fatal-rejects bind/graft with rationale "no Cloud Build, no reliquary" |
| **`RBS0:1163`** | "Yokes a reliquary stamp into a **conjure vessel's** {rbrv_regime}" |
| **`rbtdro_onboarding.rs:99-109`** | "bind mode does not use the reliquary" — excluded from yoke fan-out |

The foundry requires a value that the yoke is forbidden from setting. The orchestration's bind-mode-yoke-exclusion is the proximate cause of the failure, but it was correctly following the spec; the spec is wrong.

## The Spec Error

**RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc:31-44** is empirically false:
- "bind vessels … (no Cloud Build, no reliquary)" — bind submits a combined Cloud Build job (`zrbfd_mirror_submit`) using **skopeo** from the reliquary as the mirror step image and the about-steps (gcloud/docker/syft tools, all from reliquary) as steps 1-4.
- "graft vessels … (no Cloud Build, no reliquary)" — graft submits a combined about+vouch Cloud Build job (`zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit`) using gcloud/docker/alpine/syft from the reliquary.

This is contradicted within RBS0 itself at line 1295 ("Conjure and **bind** embed these steps in their primary Cloud Build job alongside image production; graft's primary job consists of these steps alone") and at line 1838.

## Recommended Fix — Story A (align everything to the implementation)

Treat the reliquary as the canonical builder toolset for **any** ordain-path Cloud Build, not just conjure. Cheapest change set, ships BBAAs to green:

1. **`rbfl_yoke` (rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:212-217)** — collapse the case to single-arm: any ordain-path vessel (`conjure|bind|graft`) accepts yoke. The rejection clause was wrong; remove it.
2. **`rbtdro_onboarding.rs:99-109`** — remove the bind-mode-exclusion comment; add `RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_PLANTUML_BOTTLE` to `RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS`. (Graft-demo isn't in the onboarding fixture — defer that.)
3. **`RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc`** — strike steps 31-44 (mode gate). Replace the NOTE at line 4 to drop "conjure vessel" qualifier; tool images are needed by every ordain-path Cloud Build, not just conjure.
4. **`RBS0:1163`** — change "into a conjure vessel's" to "into an ordain-path vessel's" (or similar — match whatever phrasing matches RBSDY's update).

Side effects worth checking before committing:
- The plantuml vessel's existing `RBRV_RELIQUARY=` (empty) is fine — yoke writes through.
- The graft-demo vessel: same. (Not exercised in this fixture but will need same treatment when its case lands.)
- `four-mode` fixture: probably already passes against a yoked plantuml — worth re-checking after change.

## Why not Story B/C

Story B (refactor about/vouch/mirror to not depend on reliquary) is a much larger change — touches step assembly, requires alternate sourcing for tool images for bind/graft, and has no observed need behind it. The reliquary-as-canonical-builder posture is what the implementation has been steadily reinforcing; the spec lagged.

Story C (drop RBRV_RELIQUARY field from bind/graft rbrv.env) is essentially Story B — it requires the field-less modes to source tool images differently, which they don't.

## Pause

Awaiting your guidance — Story A is the recommendation, but the call is yours. If A is approved, the fix is mechanical (4 sites listed) and I'd run the fixture once more to confirm `ordain-bind`, `ordain-pluml`, `ordain-graft` clear.

message.content: State clearly why bind needs reliquary

content.text:
## Why bind needs the reliquary

The bind operation submits one Cloud Build job (`zrbfd_mirror_submit`) with **five build steps**, and every step's container image is drawn from the reliquary:

| # | Step file | Container image | Reliquary tool |
|---|-----------|-----------------|----------------|
| 0 | `rbgjm01-mirror-image.sh` | `${z_rbfc_tool_skopeo}` | **skopeo** — performs the actual upstream→GAR image copy |
| 1 | `rbgja01-discover-platforms.py` | `${z_rbfc_tool_gcloud}` | **gcloud** — enumerates platforms via crane |
| 2 | `rbgja02-syft-per-platform.sh` | `${z_rbfc_tool_docker}` (with `${ZRBF_TOOL_SYFT}` substituted to `${z_rbfc_tool_syft}`) | **docker + syft** — generates SBOMs |
| 3 | `rbgja03-build-info-per-platform.py` | `${z_rbfc_tool_gcloud}` | **gcloud** — composes build_info.json |
| 4 | `rbgja04-assemble-push-about.sh` | `${z_rbfc_tool_docker}` | **docker** — assembles and pushes the about package |

Every `name:` field on every step is `${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}…/${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${RBRV_RELIQUARY}/«TOOL»:${RBRV_RELIQUARY}`. With `RBRV_RELIQUARY` empty, no step image can even be referenced — the build resource cannot be stitched. (Then the standalone vouch job that follows adds three more reliquary-tool steps: alpine + gcloud + docker.)

Three reasons the reliquary is the only source of these tools, not upstream:

1. **Trust boundary.** Builder tooling is the outermost layer of the supply chain. Centralizing it in GAR under content-addressed datestamps means every Cloud Build job — conjure, bind, graft, enshrine — runs the same vetted toolset, version-locked per depot.
2. **Air-gap reachability.** Bind vessels can run with `RBRV_EGRESS_MODE=airgap`, which routes the build to the airgap pool. The airgap pool has no path to Docker Hub or any public registry; the only image source it can reach is the depot's own GAR. Tool images sourced from upstream public images would simply fail to pull.
3. **Mode-symmetric about/vouch pipeline.** The about pipeline (`rbgja*`) and the vouch pipeline (`rbgjv*`) are shared across all three modes verbatim. They live in `Tools/rbk/rbgja/` and `Tools/rbk/rbgjv/` and are invoked by `zrbfc_assemble_about_steps` / `zrbfc_assemble_vouch_steps`, both of which read `${z_rbfc_tool_*}` set by `zrbfc_resolve_tool_images`. Any per-mode divergence of tool sourcing would fork the about/vouch pipeline into mode-specific copies.

So the spec's "bind vessels pin an upstream digest (no Cloud Build, no reliquary)" conflates two true facts (bind doesn't *build* an image from source, and bind's payload comes from upstream) with two false ones (no Cloud Build, no reliquary). Bind has a Cloud Build job; that job is composed exclusively of reliquary-tool steps.

message.content: Your option above recommended even making graft require reliquary.  Is that sensible?  Do you anticipate reasons graft might grow to need tools from reliquary?

content.text:
## Is it sensible? Yes — graft already requires it today, and the forces all point toward more dependence, not less.

### Today

`rbfd_graft` calls `zrbfc_resolve_tool_images` (line 1665) for the same reason bind does: the about+vouch Cloud Build job that runs after the local image push consists entirely of reliquary-tool steps:

| Step | Image | Tool |
|------|-------|------|
| about-1 (discover-platforms) | gcloud | reliquary |
| about-2 (syft-per-platform) | docker + syft | reliquary |
| about-3 (build-info-per-platform) | gcloud | reliquary |
| about-4 (assemble-push-about) | docker | reliquary |
| vouch-1 (download-verifier) | alpine | reliquary |
| vouch-2 (verify-provenance — GRAFTED stamp for graft) | gcloud | reliquary |
| vouch-3 (assemble-push-vouch) | docker | reliquary |

The only thing graft *doesn't* need from the reliquary is `skopeo` (since there's no upstream copy step). The other five tools are mandatory.

### Forces of evolution

Realistic ways the metadata pipeline could grow over time, all of which **add** reliquary dependence:

1. **Signing.** A GRAFTED stamp without a signed attestation is weaker than what conjure produces. If the project ever wants to put even minimal tamper-evidence on graft hallmarks, it brings in cosign / openssl / attestor keys — same toolchain pattern as conjure's vouch.
2. **Vulnerability scanning.** Trivy/grype/anchore added to the about pipeline are reliquary candidates by construction (same shape as syft).
3. **Multi-platform graft.** Currently graft is single-platform. If a graft vessel ever grows to push a manifest list (legitimate ask for cross-arch tooling), that's crane/skopeo work — reliquary-resident.
4. **Provenance for the graft event itself.** The "who/when/from-where" of a graft is metadata the foundry doesn't currently capture. If it did, that's a Python step running in gcloud — reliquary-resident.
5. **Reliquary expansion.** The canonical six-tool set is a current snapshot, not a frozen contract. If a seventh tool joins (e.g., cosign, crane standalone), every about/vouch step in every mode picks it up through `zrbfc_assemble_about_steps`. Graft would inherit it whether graft "needed" it or not, because the pipeline is shared.

### The one counterforce — and why it doesn't bite

The legitimate concern is **dev-cycle friction**: if graft is the iterate-locally mode, requiring depot-side reliquary inscription before graft works adds a setup step. But:

- The canonical onboarding sequence already inscribes the reliquary as case 1, before any ordain runs. The gauntlet handles this by construction.
- Solo graft against an established depot (the realistic dev-loop case) requires a reliquary that's already been inscribed once. Operators don't re-inscribe per graft.
- The alternative — sourcing tool images from upstream public registries during dev-loop builds — would *fork the about/vouch pipeline*, making graft's metadata format diverge from conjure's and bind's. That's a much worse property than "graft needs the depot to have a reliquary."

### Pipeline symmetry as the real argument

The strongest reason `rbfl_yoke` should accept graft isn't "graft happens to need reliquary today." It's that the about/vouch pipeline is **mode-uniform by design** — `rbgja*` and `rbgjv*` are written once, parameterized by mode-specific substitutions, and consumed by all three modes verbatim. Every reliquary tool the about/vouch pipeline ever uses, every mode uses. Mode-dependent reliquary requirements would be a regression of that property.

So: yes, Story A is sensible for graft. Empirically required today; structurally aligned with pipeline symmetry; the foreseeable forces add reliquary dependence rather than removing it.

content.text:
I don't disagree — the implementation has been pushing this direction for a while; this just makes it explicit. But the proposal as stated is **not quite enough** to repair the immediate failure. Let me lay out what's already there, what your proposal adds, and what the minimum repair set actually needs.

## What already exists

The "verify reliquary is in GAR before Cloud Build" check is already implemented and already runs in conjure and bind:

- `zrbfd_preflight_reliquary` (rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:107) — HEAD-checks all six canonical tool images (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft, binfmt, skopeo) against GAR.
- Called via `zrbfd_registry_preflight` (line 276), which is in turn called by `rbfd_build` (line 1156) and `rbfd_mirror` (line 1424).

But two gaps:

1. **`zrbfd_preflight_reliquary:115-116`** has an early-return on empty RBRV_RELIQUARY:
   ```bash
   local -r z_reliquary="${RBRV_RELIQUARY:-}"
   test -n "${z_reliquary}" || return 0
   ```
   If reliquary is now universally required, this early-return is wrong (should die, or be removed since `zrbfc_resolve_tool_images` already dies earlier).

2. **`rbfd_graft` doesn't call `zrbfd_registry_preflight`** (verify with grep — it's worth checking before committing). If true, graft skips the GAR-presence check entirely and relies on Cloud Build to fail. That's a real preflight gap, not just a spec gap.

## What the proposal alone repairs

Your two changes (universalize reliquary; require GAR-validity preflight) align the **spec** with what the implementation already does for conjure/bind. They do **not** unblock `ordain-bind` on the next fixture run, because the proximate failure isn't "the preflight didn't run" — it's "yoke refused to write the field at all, and orchestration colluded with the spec-error to skip writing it."

## Minimum repair set to clear the failure

1. **`rbfl_yoke` (rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:212-217)** — collapse the case so any ordain-path mode accepts yoke. Currently dies on bind/graft.
2. **`rbtdro_onboarding.rs:99-117`** — drop the bind/graft exclusion comments; add `rbev-bottle-plantuml` to `RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS`. (Graft-demo not exercised in this fixture; deferred.)
3. **`RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc:31-44`** — strike the "Gate on Vessel Mode" step. Replace the NOTE at line 4 to drop "conjure vessel" qualifier.
4. **`RBS0:1163`** — broaden "into a conjure vessel's" to "into an ordain-path vessel's" (or your preferred phrasing).
5. **`zrbfd_preflight_reliquary:115-116`** — remove the empty-RBRV_RELIQUARY early-return. (Per your ask: make absence a hard fail at preflight.)
6. **`rbfd_graft`** — add `zrbfd_registry_preflight` call if missing. (Verify before changing.)
7. **Spec update for preflight** — RBSAC (ark_conjure), RBSAG (ark_graft), and the bind ark spec (RBSAB? — bind shares about with conjure) should declare the GAR-validity preflight as a precondition step. RBS0's enshrine description already references it; conjure/bind/graft procedural specs should match.

Items 1-4 are the actual blocker fixes. Items 5-7 implement your proposal and tighten the contract.

## Worth confirming before committing
